title: What now
wikitext:
[]: What now?             Quote Post by master0rolando » Sun Nov 19, 2006 4:54 am i always was curious as to what Asuka and Shinji were gonna do after 3-I the cant just lay on the beach forever... just thinking little questions like: what do they eat?...where do they sleep? i mean there has to be debris...remnants of an old soda machine..and a little cave type thing out of a crumbled building...then again it may have been wiped out by Rei impact... (theres a funny doujin-(non-hentai even) where Asuka suggests they eat GNR..."Wondermeat") i just always looked at it and thought to myself that they would realistically, since theyre kids and stuff...starve to death...or die of disease...

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Sun Nov 19, 2006 5:03 am master0rolando wrote: (theres a funny doujin-(non-hentai even) where Asuka suggests they eat GNR..."Wondermeat")

[]: Re: What now?             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Nov 19, 2006 5:38 am master0rolando wrote: i always was curious as to what Asuka and Shinji were gonna do after 3-I

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Sun Nov 19, 2006 6:03 am For the sake of being a sarcastic ass something.

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Sun Nov 19, 2006 6:11 am You're not supposed to think about it beyond what you saw.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Nov 19, 2006 6:13 am Grave markers: Rusty nail: Obviously not all that recent additions to the scenery. Thus Shinji has been back awhile.

[]: Re: What now?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Nov 19, 2006 6:30 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Didn't it seem slow to anyone?

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Sun Nov 19, 2006 6:33 am Szmitten wrote: You're not supposed to think about it beyond what you saw.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Nov 19, 2006 7:05 am jcmoorehead wrote: It's safe to assume Tokyo-3 has been obliterated so there goes that source of supplies.

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Sun Nov 19, 2006 7:48 am Mr. Tines wrote: jcmoorehead wrote: It's safe to assume Tokyo-3 has been obliterated so there goes that source of supplies.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Nov 19, 2006 8:09 am jcmoorehead wrote: In other words, they're buggered?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Nov 19, 2006 9:25 am Your view on EVERYTHING is negative. Do you seriously think Yui would miss such an obvious problem as GNR? She planned everything else out so well that a slip of that magnitude seems unlikely. Then there is that blurriness around GNR. Would it be much of a feat for her to minimize the damage with her nearly cosmic powers?

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Sun Nov 19, 2006 10:13 am Well I can understand why his view is slightly negative, no matter what Yui's words were the fact that they return in the middle of nowhere being looked at by a giant head doesn't exactly fill me with hope. Although I guess she could just make a giant ham appear, ham solves everything.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Nov 19, 2006 10:22 am I just look at what is in the show and tell what I see as the (to coin a phrase) inconvenient truth. The devastation of the Hakone area for miles (likely tens of miles) around is a given, when the ~8mile across GeoFront is ripped from the Earth, just from the shedding of the overburden. That's before we get into side-effects like the ocean pouring into a hole that's just been ripped into a volcanic region on the edge of the tectonic plate. All this is before Yui's Ascension. Having only seen the descent of Shekinah in wonderful Mangle-vision, I can't comment on AT-field effects. Even so, we do see impacts at the order of miles per second, and the raising of miles-high splashes that would by themselves cause tsunamis sufficient to devastate at least the whole Pacific Rim, and probably low-lying regions world wide, just like Second Impact.

[]: Re: What now?             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Nov 19, 2006 10:42 am master0rolando wrote: what do they eat?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Nov 19, 2006 10:47 am master0rolando wrote: i dont see Shinji as having been there for any longer than we saw...

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Sun Nov 19, 2006 10:49 am I think Shinji has maybe been there for a day at least, the question is how to they return. Do they climb out of the LCL Ocean or do they just materialize Star Trek Style?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Nov 19, 2006 10:56 am jcmoorehead wrote: Do they climb out of the LCL Ocean or do they just materialize Star Trek Style?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 19, 2006 11:02 am Shin-seiki wrote: The broken grave marker seems to imply that "Asuka-kicks-down-her-grave" happened shortly before what we see on-screen in 'I need you', which, again, implies that Shinji had been alone up to that point.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Nov 19, 2006 11:20 am While I agree that Earth is likely to be changed forever by 3I (just like with 1I and 2I), I disagree with Mr. Tines' view on the grounds that, from a literary perspective, having you go through all of the main character's complexes (the resolution of which being the main point of the narrative), it would be ridiculous to end it with "yeah, now that you've seen Shinji come to terms with himself, he's going to die offscreen out of starvation and disease, effectively making the entirety of the story completely and absolutely pointless and denying whatever message Evangelion was intended to have".

Article: What now
 of miles) around is a given, when the ~8mile across GeoFront is ripped from the Earth, just from the shedding of the overburden. That's before we get into side-effects like the ocean pouring into a hole that's just been ripped into a volcanic region on the edge of the tectonic plate. All this is before Yui's Ascension. Having only seen the descent of Shekinah in wonderful Mangle-vision, I can't comment on AT-field effects. Even so, we do see impacts at the order of miles per second, and the raising of miles-high splashes that would by themselves cause tsunamis sufficient to devastate at least the whole Pacific Rim, and probably low-lying regions world wide, just like Second Impact.

Article: What now
 Sun Nov 19, 2006 11:20 am While I agree that Earth is likely to be changed forever by 3I (just like with 1I and 2I), I disagree with Mr. Tines' view on the grounds that, from a literary perspective, having you go through all of the main character's complexes (the resolution of which being the main point of the narrative), it would be ridiculous to end it with "yeah, now that you've seen Shinji come to terms with himself, he's going to die offscreen out of starvation and disease, effectively making the entirety of the story completely and absolutely pointless and denying whatever message Evangelion was intended to have".
